response:
```python
import pytest
from your_module import is_valid_email  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual module name

@pytest.mark.parametrize("email, expected", [
    # Happy path: Valid emails
    ("test@example.com", True),
    ("user.name+tag@example.co.uk", True),
    ("a@b.co", True),  # Minimal valid email
    ("123@example.org", True),
    ("test_123@example-domain.com", True),
    ("user%tag@example.net", True),
    
    # Edge cases: Boundary conditions
    ("a@b.c", True),  # Shortest possible TLD (2 chars)
    ("test@sub.domain.com", True),  # Subdomain
    ("very.long.email.address@very.long.domain.name.com", True),  # Long email
    ("test@example.io", True),  # TLD with 2 chars
    ("test@example.travel", True),  # TLD with more than 2 chars
    
    # Error cases: Invalid emails
    ("", False),  # Empty string
    ("invalid", False),  # No @ symbol
    ("test@", False),  # No domain
    ("@example.com", False),  # No local part
    ("test@example", False),  # No TLD
    ("test@example.c", False),  # TLD too short (1 char)
    ("test@exam ple.com", False),  # Space in domain
    ("test @example.com", False),  # Space in local part
    ("test@@example.com", False),  # Double @
    ("test@example..com", False),  # Double dot in domain
    ("test@.com", False),  # Domain starts with dot
    ("test@example.", False),  # Domain ends with dot
    ("test@example.com.", False),  # Trailing dot
    (".test@example.com", False),  # Local part starts with dot
    ("test.@example.com", False),  # Local part ends with dot
    ("test..test@example.com", False),  # Double dot in local part
    ("test@example_com", False),  # Underscore in domain (not allowed in this pattern)
    ("test@exam!ple.com", False),  # Invalid char in domain
    ("tes!t@example.com", False),  # Invalid char in local part (only allowed: a-zA-Z0-9._%+-)
])
def test_is_valid_email(email, expected):
    assert is_valid_email(email) == expected
```
